focus: Gap audit — coverage confirmation or missed patterns

description: Per-iteration pattern coverage audit table (iters 1-10) showing 100% completeness across all 5 RQs: RQ1 (5 baseline + 4 deepening = 9 artifacts), RQ2 (5 baseline + 4 deepening = 9 artifacts), RQ3 (5 baseline + 4 deepening = 9 artifacts), RQ4 (5 baseline + 4 deepening = 9 artifacts), RQ5 (1 synthesis + 4 cross-cutting = 5 artifacts). Total: 41 patterns/artifacts with smallcode provenance citations, candidate target paths, patch shapes, and acceptance criteria. No gaps identified.

Outcome B: Coverage confirmed. No high-value patterns missed from smallcode for the 5 RQs in scope. The iterations 1-10 have comprehensively covered context budget engine, output verification pipeline, per-model profiles & escalation, structured scope/permissions, and skill architecture synthesis. Dropped-RQ patterns (tool routing, auto-decompose) are excluded per ADR-002 trim for SCOPE reasons (overlaps with mcp-code-mode and sk-prompt) and have LOW-MEDIUM small-model output-quality contribution relative to in-scope RQs.

Synthesis structure for research.md: Section 1 (Executive Summary), Section 2 (Per-RQ Pattern Catalog RQ1-4 with baseline + deepening artifacts), Section 3 (Architecture Synthesis RQ5 + Cross-Cutting), Section 4 (Implementation Priority Ranking: P0 AGENTS.md + graph-metadata, P1 cli-devin references, P2 cli-opencode permissions matrix, P3 shared assets, P4 sk-prompt integration), Section 5 (Integration Verification Plan with advisor routing tests and RM-8 counter-example validation), Section 6 (Dropped-RQ Rationale Appendix), Section 7 (Citations Index). Ordering rationale: RQ1→RQ2→RQ3→RQ4 follows small-model dispatch lifecycle (budget → verify → profile → permissions).
